_note: The agent's entire interface to the world. Every action is one event that changes the world; add a verb by adding an entry here.

classes.observe: zero-duration, no mutation; returns a view scoped to the agent. Does not move the clock.
classes.mutate: zero-duration; synchronous state change + immediate ack; may schedule follow-up events. Does not move the clock.
classes.advance: carries a duration; releases the clock to now + duration, firing all due events in time order. The only class that moves time.

_write_policy: No action can write derived or graded fields (e.g. blockers.*.surfaced, tasks.*.blocked_by, decision correctness). Those change only through the world's own rules or an NPC reveal — the anti-gaming backbone.

actions.effect: append message to a chat channel
actions.emits: npc_reply at now + the persona's response_delay; the body is mapped to one intent
actions.pre: channel exists and agent is a member, or a DM to a known person
actions._note: refs are optional structured pointers (e.g. a blocker id) the grader reads deterministically, without trusting the prose

actions.emits: npc_reply at now + the persona's response_delay

actions._note: status/owner/due/priority are writable; blocked_by is DERIVED and not writable — editing status does not clear a blocker (touches no graded field)

actions.effect: append doc (a free-text artifact eligible for extraction)

actions._note: the reward-bearing structured action — grading reads these fields directly; prefer it over free-text docs so scoring reads structure

actions.effect: release the clock to now + duration; the next-event jump fires all due events in time order
actions._note: the canonical clock-release; carries no mutation of its own

actions.effect: release the clock across the meeting window
actions.pre: agent is an attendee and now is within the meeting window
